author: Sofia Martínez
title: Tourism SEO in the AI Era: How to Get Recommended by AI Travel Assistants
description: AI travel assistants are replacing the 'best hotels near me' Google search. Tourism businesses that optimize for AI recommendations are capturing bookings before users reach OTAs.

sections.p: Travel planning has moved to AI faster than almost any other category. Travelers ask ChatGPT and Perplexity 'what should I do in [city] for 3 days?' and get complete itineraries with specific recommendations. The businesses cited in those itineraries get direct bookings. The ones not cited lose those customers to competitors or OTAs.

sections.h2: How AI travel assistants choose what to recommend

sections.p: AI travel recommendations are built from web content, review platforms, and structured data. A hotel or tour operator that has a well-maintained website with clear descriptions of the experience, a strong review profile across multiple platforms, and proper schema markup is significantly more likely to appear in AI-generated travel itineraries than a competitor with a weak web presence — regardless of actual quality.
The uncomfortable truth: AI can't visit your property. It only knows what the web says about it. Investing in your digital presence is investing in how AI systems represent you to travelers.

sections.h2: The content strategy for tourism businesses

list: Experience-specific landing pages: each tour, experience, or package gets its own page with full description, duration, what's included, and ideal traveler profile.
Local area guides: content about the destination that positions your business as the local expert.
Itinerary integration: content framed as 'how to spend [X days] in [location]' that naturally includes your business.
Seasonal content: updated content for high-season queries before search volume peaks.

sections.p: Tourism AI recommendations pull from TripAdvisor, Google, Booking.com, and Airbnb Experiences. A tourism business needs a review strategy across all relevant platforms, not just one. Google reviews drive Map Pack; TripAdvisor reviews drive AI recommendations; Booking.com drives OTA visibility. Each plays a different role.

Every traveler who books through an OTA costs you 15–25% in commission. Every traveler who books direct because they found you through AI search costs you nothing but the marketing investment. Tourism businesses with strong AI search visibility are systematically increasing their direct booking percentage — the most profitable shift in the industry.

sections.quote: The tourism business that AI recommends first doesn't need to fight for OTA rankings. It just needs to be easy to book directly.
